title: Getting Started with pitlaneR
text: f1_seasons() returns every F1 season available database.

title: Getting Started with pitlaneR
text: f1_races() returns race calendar given season, including circuit details session times. defaults current year.
code: # Current season f1_races()  # A specific season f1_races(2024)

title: Getting Started with pitlaneR
text: f1_circuits() returns circuit information, including geographic coordinates. Pass season see circuits used year, omit circuits database.
code: # Circuits used in 2024 f1_circuits(2024)  # All circuits ever used in F1 f1_circuits()

title: Getting Started with pitlaneR
text: f1_drivers() f1_constructors() return reference information drivers teams. accept optional season filter participants year.
code: # Drivers who raced in 2024 f1_drivers(2024)  # All constructors in the database f1_constructors()

title: Getting Started with pitlaneR
text: f1_results() returns finishing results season, specific round within season. row represents one driver’s result one race. returned tibble includes grid position, finishing position, points, status, driver constructor details, (available) fastest lap information.
code: # All results for the 2024 season f1_results(2024)  # Just round 1 f1_results(2024, round = 1)

title: Getting Started with pitlaneR
text: f1_qualifying() returns qualifying session results, including Q1, Q2, Q3 lap times. Note: Qualifying data available 1994 season onward. Requesting earlier season raise error.

title: Getting Started with pitlaneR
text: f1_sprint() returns results sprint races, introduced 2021 season. function returns empty tibble seasons rounds without sprints.

title: Getting Started with pitlaneR
text: f1_driver_standings() f1_constructor_standings() return championship table given season. default return final (latest available) standings; pass round number see standings specific point season.
code: # Final 2024 drivers' championship f1_driver_standings(2024)  # Constructors' standings after round 10 f1_constructor_standings(2024, round = 10)

title: Getting Started with pitlaneR
text: f1_laps() returns lap--lap timing data specific race. season round required. can optionally request single lap. Note: Lap time data available 1996 season onward. endpoint can return large volume data; progress bar shown multi-page fetches.
code: # All laps from the 2024 Bahrain GP (round 1) f1_laps(2024, round = 1)  # Just the first lap f1_laps(2024, round = 1, lap = 1)

title: Getting Started with pitlaneR
text: f1_pit_stops() returns pit stop data specific race, including stop number, lap, time day, duration. Note: Pit stop data available 2012 season onward.

title: Getting Started with pitlaneR
text: API responses cached memory one hour avoid redundant requests. need fresh data (e.g. live race weekend), clear cache:

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ben Jackman. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Jackman B (2026). pitlaneR: Access Formula 1 Data Jolpica API. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/benjackman/pitlaneR.
code: @Manual{,   title = {pitlaneR: Access Formula 1 Data from the Jolpica API},   author = {Ben Jackman},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/benjackman/pitlaneR}, }

what: Access Formula 1 Data from the Jolpica API
title: Access Formula 1 Data from the Jolpica API
text: Access Formula 1 data R using free Jolpica API (Ergast-compatible). API key Python dependency required. Important: current version R package largely produce Claude, part Posit’s AI beta. code partially tested, treated caution. Read process blog.

title: Access Formula 1 Data from the Jolpica API
Access Formula 1 Data from the Jolpica API
code: library(pitlaneR)  # Race results for a specific round f1_results(2024, 1)  # Full season results f1_results(2024)  # Current championship standings f1_driver_standings(2024) f1_constructor_standings(2024)  # Qualifying results f1_qualifying(2024, 1)  # Drivers and constructors f1_drivers(2024) f1_constructors(2024)  # Race schedule f1_races(2024)  # Circuit info f1_circuits()  # Lap times (available from 1996+) f1_laps(2024, 1)  # Pit stops (available from 2012+) f1_pit_stops(2024, 1)  # Sprint results f1_sprint(2024)  # All F1 seasons f1_seasons()

title: Access Formula 1 Data from the Jolpica API
text: Tidy output: functions return tibbles snake_case column names Automatic pagination: Large result sets fetched across multiple API pages automatically Session caching: Repeated calls served -memory cache (reset f1_clear_cache()) Historical coverage: Data 1950 present season

title: Access Formula 1 Data from the Jolpica API
text: data comes Jolpica F1 API, free, community-maintained successor Ergast Motor Racing API.

title: Access Formula 1 Data from the Jolpica API
text: MIT

title: Get F1 circuit information — f1_circuits
text: Retrieves circuit information, optionally filtered circuits used specific season.

title: Get F1 circuit information — f1_circuits
text: season Integer character. season year (e.g., 2024). NULL (default), returns circuits database.

title: Get F1 circuit information — f1_circuits
text: tibble one row per circuit, including columns: circuit_id Character. Unique circuit identifier. circuit_name Character. Full circuit name. location_locality Character. City locality. location_country Character. Country. location_lat Numeric. Latitude. location_long Numeric. Longitude. url Character. Wikipedia URL.

title: Get F1 circuit information — f1_circuits
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # All circuits in the database f1_circuits()  # Circuits used in a specific season f1_circuits(2024) } # }

what: Clear the pitlaneR session cache — f1_clear_cache
title: Clear the pitlaneR session cache — f1_clear_cache
text: Removes cached API responses current R session. API responses cached memory one hour reduce redundant requests. Use function force fresh data API subsequent calls.

title: Clear the pitlaneR session cache — f1_clear_cache
Clear the pitlaneR session cache — f1_clear_cache
text: Invisible NULL. success message printed via cli::cli_alert_success().

title: Clear the pitlaneR session cache — f1_clear_cache

what: Get F1 constructor championship standings — f1_constructor_standings
title: Get F1 constructor championship standings — f1_constructor_standings
text: Retrieves World Constructors' Championship standings given season, optionally specific round. Constructors' Championship awarded since 1958.

title: Get F1 constructor championship standings — f1_constructor_standings
Get F1 constructor championship standings — f1_constructor_standings
text: season Integer character. season year (e.g., 2024). Defaults current year. round Integer character. round number. NULL (default), returns final (latest available) standings season.

title: Get F1 constructor championship standings — f1_constructor_standings
text: tibble one row per constructor, including columns: season Integer. Season year. standings_round Integer. Round number standings apply. position Integer. Championship position. points Numeric. Total championship points. wins Integer. Number race wins. constructor_* Constructor details (id, name, nationality).

title: Get F1 constructor championship standings — f1_constructor_standings
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Final standings for a season f1_constructor_standings(2024)  # Standings after round 5 f1_constructor_standings(2024, 5) } # }

title: Get F1 constructor information — f1_constructors
text: Retrieves constructor (team) information, optionally filtered constructors participated specific season.

title: Get F1 constructor information — f1_constructors
text: season Integer character. season year (e.g., 2024). NULL (default), returns constructors database.

title: Get F1 constructor information — f1_constructors
text: tibble one row per constructor, including columns: constructor_id Character. Unique constructor identifier. name Character. Constructor name. nationality Character. Constructor nationality. url Character. Wikipedia URL.

title: Get F1 constructor information — f1_constructors
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Constructors from a specific season f1_constructors(2024)  # All constructors in the database f1_constructors() } # }

what: Get F1 driver championship standings — f1_driver_standings
title: Get F1 driver championship standings — f1_driver_standings
text: Retrieves World Drivers' Championship standings given season, optionally specific round.

title: Get F1 driver championship standings — f1_driver_standings
Get F1 driver championship standings — f1_driver_standings
text: season Integer character. season year (e.g., 2024). Defaults current year. round Integer character. round number. NULL (default), returns final (latest available) standings season.

title: Get F1 driver championship standings — f1_driver_standings
text: tibble one row per driver, including columns: season Integer. Season year. standings_round Integer. Round number standings apply. position Integer. Championship position. points Numeric. Total championship points. wins Integer. Number race wins. driver_Driver details (id, code, given/family name, etc.). constructor_ Constructor details driver's team.

title: Get F1 driver championship standings — f1_driver_standings
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Final standings for a season f1_driver_standings(2024)  # Standings after round 5 f1_driver_standings(2024, 5) } # }

title: Get F1 driver information — f1_drivers
text: Retrieves driver information, optionally filtered drivers participated specific season.

title: Get F1 driver information — f1_drivers
text: season Integer character. season year (e.g., 2024). NULL (default), returns drivers database.

title: Get F1 driver information — f1_drivers
text: tibble one row per driver, including columns: driver_id Character. Unique driver identifier. permanent_number Integer. Driver's permanent car number. code Character. Three-letter driver code (e.g., "VER"). given_name Character. Driver's first name. family_name Character. Driver's last name. date_of_birth Date. Driver's date birth. nationality Character. Driver's nationality. url Character. Wikipedia URL.

title: Get F1 driver information — f1_drivers
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Drivers from a specific season f1_drivers(2024)  # All drivers in the database f1_drivers() } # }

what: Get F1 lap time data — f1_laps
title: Get F1 lap time data — f1_laps
text: Retrieves lap--lap timing data specific race. endpoint can return large amount data; progress bar shown multi-page fetches.

title: Get F1 lap time data — f1_laps
Get F1 lap time data — f1_laps
text: season Integer character. season year (e.g., 2024). Required (default). round Integer character. round number. Required (default). lap Integer character. specific lap number. NULL (default), returns timing data laps race.

title: Get F1 lap time data — f1_laps
text: tibble one row per driver per lap, including columns: season Integer. Season year. round Integer. Round number. race_name Character. Name Grand Prix. lap Integer. Lap number. driver_id Character. Unique driver identifier. position Integer. Position end lap. time Character. Lap time.

title: Get F1 lap time data — f1_laps
text: Lap time data available 1996 season onward.

title: Get F1 lap time data — f1_laps
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # All lap times for a race f1_laps(2024, 1)  # First lap only f1_laps(2024, 1, lap = 1) } # }

what: Get F1 pit stop data — f1_pit_stops
title: Get F1 pit stop data — f1_pit_stops
text: Retrieves pit stop data specific race, including stop number, lap, time day, duration.

title: Get F1 pit stop data — f1_pit_stops
Get F1 pit stop data — f1_pit_stops
text: season Integer character. season year (e.g., 2024). Required (default). round Integer character. round number. Required (default).

title: Get F1 pit stop data — f1_pit_stops
text: tibble one row per pit stop, including columns: season Integer. Season year. round Integer. Round number. race_name Character. Name Grand Prix. driver_id Character. Unique driver identifier. stop Integer. Pit stop number driver (1st, 2nd, etc.). lap Integer. Lap pit stop occurred. time Character. Time day pit stop. duration Character. Duration pit stop.

title: Get F1 pit stop data — f1_pit_stops
text: Pit stop data available 2012 season onward.

title: Get F1 pit stop data — f1_pit_stops
Get F1 qualifying results — f1_qualifying
text: Retrieves qualifying session results given season optionally specific round. Qualifying data available 1994 season onward; requesting earlier seasons raise error.

title: Get F1 qualifying results — f1_qualifying
text: season Integer character. season year (e.g., 2024), must 1994 later (Jolpica API provide qualifying results earlier 1994). Defaults current year. round Integer character. round number within season. NULL (default), returns qualifying results rounds season.

title: Get F1 qualifying results — f1_qualifying
text: tibble one row per driver per qualifying session, including columns: season Integer. Season year. round Integer. Round number. race_name Character. Name Grand Prix. race_date Date. Date race. position Integer. Final qualifying position. q1, q2, q3 Character. Lap times set qualifying session. Missing driver participate session. driver_Driver details (id, code, given/family name, etc.). constructor_ Constructor details (id, name, nationality).

title: Get F1 qualifying results — f1_qualifying
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Qualifying for a specific race f1_qualifying(2024, 1)  # All qualifying results for a season f1_qualifying(2024) } # }

title: Get F1 race schedule — f1_races
text: Retrieves race schedule given season, including circuit information, dates, session times.

title: Get F1 race schedule — f1_races
text: season Integer character. season year (e.g., 2024). Defaults current year.

title: Get F1 race schedule — f1_races
text: tibble one row per race, including columns: season Integer. Season year. round Integer. Round number. race_name Character. Name Grand Prix. date Date. Date race. time Character. Scheduled start time (UTC). url Character. Wikipedia URL race. circuit_Circuit details (id, name, location, coordinates). first_practice_ First practice session date time. qualifying_Qualifying session date time. sprint_ Sprint session date time (applicable).

title: Get F1 race schedule — f1_races
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Race schedule for a specific season f1_races(2024)  # Current season schedule f1_races() } # }

title: Get F1 race results — f1_results
text: Retrieves race results given season optionally specific round.

title: Get F1 race results — f1_results
text: season Integer character. season year (e.g., 2024). Defaults current year. round Integer character. round number within season. NULL (default), returns results rounds season.

title: Get F1 race results — f1_results
text: tibble one row per driver per race, including columns: season Integer. Season year. round Integer. Round number. race_name Character. Name Grand Prix. race_date Date. Date race. number Integer. Driver's car number. position Integer. Finishing position. points Numeric. Points awarded. grid Integer. Starting grid position. laps Integer. Number laps completed. status Character. Finishing status (e.g., "Finished", "+1 Lap"). driver_Driver details (id, code, given/family name, etc.). constructor_ Constructor details (id, name, nationality). time_Race time information (available). fastest_lap_ Fastest lap details (available).

title: Get F1 race results — f1_results
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Results for a specific race f1_results(2024, 1)  # All results for a season f1_results(2024) } # }

title: Get all F1 seasons — f1_seasons
text: Retrieves list Formula 1 World Championship seasons available database, covering 1950 present.

title: Get all F1 seasons — f1_seasons
text: tibble one row per season, including columns: season Integer. season year. url Character. Wikipedia URL season.

what: Get F1 sprint race results — f1_sprint
title: Get F1 sprint race results — f1_sprint
text: Retrieves sprint race results given season optionally specific round. Sprint races introduced 2021 shorter races held Saturday selected events.

title: Get F1 sprint race results — f1_sprint
Get F1 sprint race results — f1_sprint
text: season Integer character. season year (e.g., 2024). Defaults current year. round Integer character. round number within season. NULL (default), returns sprint results rounds season.

title: Get F1 sprint race results — f1_sprint
text: tibble one row per driver per sprint race, including columns: season Integer. Season year. round Integer. Round number. race_name Character. Name Grand Prix. race_date Date. Date race. number Integer. Driver's car number. position Integer. Finishing position. points Numeric. Points awarded. grid Integer. Starting grid position. laps Integer. Number laps completed. status Character. Finishing status. driver_Driver details (id, code, given/family name, etc.). constructor_ Constructor details (id, name, nationality). Returns empty tibble seasons rounds without sprint races.

title: Get F1 sprint race results — f1_sprint
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Sprint results for a specific race f1_sprint(2024, 1)  # All sprint results for a season f1_sprint(2024) } # }

what: pitlaneR: Access Formula 1 Data from the Jolpica API — pitlaneR-package
title: pitlaneR: Access Formula 1 Data from the Jolpica API — pitlaneR-package
text: Provides tidy access historical current Formula 1 data via free Jolpica API (Ergast-compatible). data returned tibbles snake_case column names automatic type conversion. API key Python dependency required.

title: pitlaneR: Access Formula 1 Data from the Jolpica API — pitlaneR-package
text: Race results: f1_results() — Race results f1_qualifying() — Qualifying session results f1_sprint() — Sprint race results Championship standings: f1_driver_standings() — Drivers' championship f1_constructor_standings() — Constructors' championship Reference data: f1_drivers() — Driver information f1_constructors() — Constructor (team) information f1_races() — Race schedule f1_circuits() — Circuit information f1_seasons() — Available seasons Detailed timing: f1_laps() — Lap--lap timing (1996+) f1_pit_stops() — Pit stop data (2012+) Caching: f1_clear_cache() — Clear cached API responses

title: pitlaneR: Access Formula 1 Data from the Jolpica API — pitlaneR-package
text: API responses cached memory duration R session (one hour). Call f1_clear_cache() force fresh requests.

title: pitlaneR: Access Formula 1 Data from the Jolpica API — pitlaneR-package
text: Data sourced Jolpica API, community-maintained successor Ergast API, covering Formula 1 World Championship 1950 present.

title: pitlaneR: Access Formula 1 Data from the Jolpica API — pitlaneR-package
text: Maintainer: Ben Jackman ben@example.com
